nameMeanings.name: Oldfield
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire and Lancashire): topographic name for someone who lived by ‘the old field’ (Middle English olde + feld) or who lived in a district that had been named in Old English as ‘the old open country’ (ald, feld). This is the source of placenames such as Allfield in Condover (Shropshire), Alfell Farm in Elmstead (Essex), Oldfield Grove in Langley (Essex), and several places in Cambridgeshire called (The) Offal, as well as many places called Oldfield in Yorkshire. Oldfield near Ludden Foot in Sowerby and another place so called in Honley, next to Meltham, are known to have given rise to hereditary surnames.

nameMeanings.name: Maude
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Maud  , the usual spelling of the name in North America.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Maude Oldfield was born about 1514, in Halifax, Yorkshire, England. She married William Mawde or Hartley about 1534, in Halifax, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 6 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Maude Oldfield est née environ 1514, à Halifax, Everwyck, Angleterre. Elle a épousé William Mawde or Hartley environ 1534, à Halifax, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 6 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Maude Oldfield wurde um 1514 in Halifax, Yorkshire, England geboren. Sie hat um 1534 in Halifax, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom William Mawde or Hartley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 6 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Maude Oldfield è nata il circa 1514 nel Halifax, Yorkshire, England. Lei sposato William Mawde or Hartley circa 1534, nel Halifax, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 6 figlie.

ko.lifeSummary: Maude Oldfield(은)는 대략 1514년 영국 요르크실르 할이파스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1534년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 할이파스에서 William Mawde or Hartley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다.
